books.title: Les Misérables
books.author: Victor Hugo
books.description: A sweeping tale of justice, love, and redemption set against the backdrop of 19th-century France. Follow Jean Valjean's journey from convict to saintly figure.

books.title: Dune
books.author: Frank Herbert
books.description: Set on the desert planet Arrakis, Dune is the story of Paul Atreides, a brilliant and gifted young man born into a great destiny beyond his understanding.

books.title: Hamlet
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.description: The Tragedy of Hamlet, Prince of Denmark, is a tragedy written by William Shakespeare. It tells the story of Prince Hamlet's quest for revenge.

books.title: The Hobbit
books.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
books.description: Bilbo Baggins is a hobbit who enjoys a comfortable, unambitious life, rarely traveling further than the pantry of his hobbit-hole in Bag End.

books.author: George Orwell
books.description: Among the seminal texts of the 20th century, Nineteen Eighty-Four is a rare work that grows more haunting as its futuristic purgatory becomes more real.

books.title: Pride and Prejudice
books.author: Jane Austen
books.description: Since its immediate success in 1813, Pride and Prejudice has remained one of the most popular novels in the English language.

books.title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
books.author: J.K. Rowling
books.description: Harry Potter has never even heard of Hogwarts when the letters start dropping on the doormat at number four, Privet Drive.

books.title: The Great Gatsby
books.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
books.description: The story of the fabulously wealthy Jay Gatsby and his love for the beautiful Daisy Buchanan, of lavish parties on Long Island.
